tools.name: files_read_file
tools.description: Read the contents of a file.

path.description: File path relative to the sandbox root (use ./ prefix)

tools.name: files_read_lines
tools.description: Read a range of lines from a file with line numbers. Returns lines in the format {line_number}|{content}. Use this instead of files_read_file when you only need a specific portion of a file to reduce context usage.

path.description: File path relative to the sandbox root (use ./ prefix)

start_line.description: Line number to start reading at (1-indexed, inclusive)

end_line.description: Line number to end reading at (1-indexed, inclusive). Defaults to start_line (single line read) if omitted.

tools.name: files_list_dir
tools.description: List files and directories at a given path. When an AGENTS.md file exists in the listed directory, its contents are automatically appended to the listing as a context section — this is not part of the ls output but an automatic context-loading feature.

path.description: Directory path relative to the sandbox root (use ./ prefix)

long.description: Show detailed format with permissions, size, and dates

tools.name: files_search_content
tools.description: Search file contents for a pattern using grep. Returns matching lines with context.

path.description: File or directory path relative to the sandbox root (use ./ prefix)

recursive.description: Search directories recursively. On by default — set to false only when searching a single file path.

files_only.description: Show only file names that contain matches, not the matching lines

tools.name: files_write_file
tools.description: Write content to a file. Creates the file if it does not exist, overwrites if it does. Parent directories are created automatically if they do not exist.

path.description: File path relative to the sandbox root (use ./ prefix)

content.description: Content to write to the file

tools.name: files_write_lines
tools.description: Replace a range of lines in a file without rewriting the entire file. Lines outside the range are preserved unchanged. Returns a diff showing removed lines (-prefix), added lines (+prefix), and surrounding context so you can verify the edit. Use this instead of files_write_file for targeted edits to large files.

path.description: File path relative to the sandbox root (use ./ prefix)

start_line.description: Line number to start replacing at (1-indexed, inclusive)

end_line.description: Line number to end replacing at (1-indexed, inclusive). Defaults to start_line (single line replacement) if omitted.

original_content.description: The content currently at [start_line, end_line]. The tool verifies this matches the file before applying the patch, using a four-stage comparison: exact match, NFC normalization, Unicode-to-ASCII folding (for smart quotes, dashes, etc.), and trailing-whitespace tolerance with automatic preservation — if the only difference is trailing whitespace, the file's original trailing whitespace is preserved in the replacement. If it doesn't match, the edit is rejected with the expected vs actual content — re-read the file and retry.

content.description: Replacement content for the specified line range

tools.name: files_replace
tools.description: Replace all occurrences of a pattern in a file. Supports capture groups ($1-$9) in the replacement string. Returns a diff of changes made. The pattern is matched against the full file content with multiline mode enabled (^ and $ match line boundaries). Use this instead of multiple files_write_lines calls when the same edit applies to many locations in one file. When the regex pattern matches 0 times, the tool automatically retries with a trailing-whitespace-tolerant literal search that ignores trailing whitespace differences between the pattern and the file content (matches must align with line boundaries). Use literal mode when the pattern contains regex metacharacters that should be matched as-is.

path.description: File path relative to the sandbox root (use ./ prefix)

pattern.description: Search pattern. By default, interpreted as regex matched against the full file content with multiline mode (^ and $ match line boundaries). In literal mode, the pattern is treated as literal text — no regex interpretation. For multi-line patterns, include actual newlines in the string.

replacement.description: Replacement string. Supports $1-$9 for capture group references (regex mode only). Use $$ for a literal $. Use an empty string to delete matches. For multi-line replacements, include actual newlines in the string.

max_replacements.description: Maximum number of replacements to apply. 0 (default) means unlimited. Use 1 to replace only the first match and avoid unintended changes when the same pattern appears in multiple locations.

literal.description: Treat the pattern as literal text instead of regex. Use this when the pattern contains regex metacharacters (especially |, (), [], ., *, +, ?, {}) that should be matched as-is — common in source code, markdown tables, JSON, and URLs. Capture groups ($1-$9) are not supported in literal mode. Default: false.

line_numbers.description: Show line numbers in the returned diff (default: true)

tools.name: files_delete_file
tools.description: Delete a file. Refuses to delete directories.

path.description: File path relative to the sandbox root (use ./ prefix)

tools.name: files_delete_dir
tools.description: Delete an empty directory. Refuses to delete non-empty directories or files.

path.description: Directory path relative to the sandbox root (use ./ prefix)
